div1.entryFree:
Midway, subst. 1) the middle way, the medium between two extremes, the mean: he were an excellent man that were made just in the m. between him and Benedick, Ado II, 1, 8. no m. 'twixt these extremes at all, Ant. III, 4, 19.
Adjectively, == being in the middle of the way: the crows and choughs that wing the m. air show scarce so gross as beetles, Lr. IV, 6, 13.
